content.text:
The test confirms: expected 65, received 80. The current code computes 3 x 25 + 5 = 80, but the block pricing rule says exactly 3 alike components should cost 60 G instead of 75 G.

The minimal implementation: check if there are exactly 3 items of the same component type, and if so, use 60 G as the block premium instead of summing individual premiums.

content.text:
All 9 tests pass (including the previously failing "3 runes" test), and 35 remain as todo.

Let me verify the final state of the implementation file.

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added component block pricing logic to `claimOffice`. Items are split into components (rune, moonstone) and non-components. When there are exactly 3 components of the same type, a flat 60 G block premium is used instead of summing individual premiums (3 x 25 = 75 G). Otherwise, components are priced individually.

**Result**: All 9 tests now pass (35 todo remaining).

**Approach**: This is minimal because:
- It only handles the single case demanded by the test: exactly 3 alike components get a 60 G block price.
- Non-block cases (1 rune, 2 runes) still fall through to the individual pricing path, keeping those tests green.
- No logic was added for future tests (4 runes no-block, mixed types, multiple blocks, etc.) -- those will be handled when their tests demand it.

**Files modified**:
- `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-21_15-29-15_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.1-testlist-scope-fix_opus-4-6-portkey-no-thinking-3/src/claim-office.ts`

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
